enrichment.short_description: Open-source e-signature platform for creating, sending, and signing documents, with cloud or self-hosted deployment options.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Documenso is an open-source e-signature platform with free, team, and self-hosted plans for signing and managing documents.

features.title: Document workflows and templates
features.description: Create reusable, directly-linkable templates to speed up repeated document signing workflows.

features.title: API and webhooks
features.description: Build custom signing automations and integrations using Documenso's API and webhook support.

features.title: Team collaboration
features.description: The Teams plan includes 5 users (expandable) and a shared team workspace for collaborative document signing.

features.title: Embedded and white-label signing
features.description: The Platform plan supports embedded signing with white-label branding for deeper product integration.

features.title: Open-source and self-hosting
features.description: Documenso's code is open source on GitHub, and organizations can self-host via Docker instead of using the hosted cloud service.

features.title: Public signer profile
features.description: Users get a public profile page, with a premium profile name available on paid plans.

features.title: Compliance certifications
features.description: Documenso states compliance with the ESIGN Act, UETA, 21 CFR Part 11, SOC2, and HIPAA.

pros: Open-source codebase available on GitHub for transparency and self-hosting
Free plan available with no credit card required (5 documents/month)
Self-hosted licensing option for full infrastructure control
API access included even on the Individual plan for personal automation
States compliance with ESIGN Act, UETA, 21 CFR Part 11, SOC2, and HIPAA
cons: Free plan is limited to 5 documents per month and 10 recipients per document
White-label and enterprise add-ons require the higher-priced Platform or Enterprise tier
Language support is not documented on the website
Company headquarters and founding year are not disclosed on the site

faqs.question: Is Documenso open source?
faqs.answer: Yes, Documenso states its code is open source and available on GitHub, and it can be self-hosted via Docker.

faqs.question: Does Documenso have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Free plan allows up to 5 documents per month with up to 10 recipients per document, and no credit card is required.

faqs.question: Can I self-host Documenso?
faqs.answer: Yes, Documenso offers a self-hosted licensing option in addition to its hosted cloud service.

faqs.question: What compliance standards does Documenso support?
faqs.answer: Documenso states support for the ESIGN Act, UETA, 21 CFR Part 11, SOC2, and HIPAA.

faqs.question: Does Documenso offer an API?
faqs.answer: Yes, API access is included starting with the Individual plan, with unlimited API access on the Platform plan.

use_cases.title: Individual document signing
use_cases.description: Individuals who need to sign or send a low volume of documents can use the free plan or the Individual plan for unlimited documents.

use_cases.title: Team contract workflows
use_cases.description: The Teams plan supports a shared workspace and embedded signing for collaborative document workflows.

use_cases.title: Custom product integration
use_cases.description: The Platform plan offers unlimited API access and white-label embedded signing for companies building signing into their own products.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted deployment
use_cases.description: Organizations that require full infrastructure control can self-host Documenso instead of using the hosted cloud version.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Documenso?
paragraphs: Documenso is an electronic signature platform for creating, sending, and signing documents online, with tools for building signing workflows and reusable templates.
The product is open source, with its code published on GitHub, and can be used as a hosted cloud service or self-hosted on your own infrastructure via Docker.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Documenso is for
paragraphs: Documenso serves individuals who need a free or low-cost way to sign occasional documents, as well as teams and platform builders who need API access, embedded signing, and white-label options.

enrichment.short_description: Open-source e-signature and document workflow platform for legally binding contracts, offering free, cloud, and self-hosted plans.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: DocuSeal is an open-source e-signature tool with a free plan, self-hosted option, and API for document signing workflows.

features.title: Document signing API
features.description: Build custom signing automations and embed document workflows into your own applications using DocuSeal's API.

features.title: Embedded and React signing
features.description: A dedicated React component lets developers embed the document signing experience directly into their apps.

features.title: Payment collection
features.description: Collect payments from signers as part of the signing workflow.

features.title: Qualified Electronic Signature
features.description: Supports Qualified Electronic Signatures for the highest level of legal trust.

features.title: Knowledge-Based Authentication
features.description: Verifies signer identity through knowledge-based authentication before signing.

features.title: Broad file format support
features.description: Supports signing workflows for PDF, DOCX, XLSX, JPEG, PNG, and ZIP files up to 100MB.

features.title: AI-native workflows
features.description: DocuSeal highlights new AI-native workflow capabilities on its site.

features.title: Self-hosted and cloud deployment
features.description: Available as a cloud-hosted service, a GDPR-compliant EU Cloud option, or a self-hosted on-premises installation.

pros: Free forever plan for individuals
Open-source on-premises option with GitHub-based support
GDPR-compliant EU Cloud deployment option available
Supports a wide range of file formats (PDF, DOCX, XLSX, JPEG, PNG, ZIP)
States it is used by 191,100+ businesses and individuals
cons: Free Basic plan is limited to 10 signature request emails and 20 SMS requests per month
SSO/SAML, bulk sending, and white-label branding require the paid Pro plan
ID verification and Qualified Electronic Signature carry additional per-use fees ($2-4)
Founding year, headquarters, and company size are not disclosed on the site

faqs.question: Is DocuSeal open source?
faqs.answer: Yes, DocuSeal offers a free, open-source on-premises plan supported through GitHub, alongside its hosted cloud plans.

faqs.question: Does DocuSeal have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Basic cloud plan is free and includes legally-binding e-signatures, indefinite document retention, and up to 10 signature request emails per month.

faqs.question: Can DocuSeal be self-hosted?
faqs.answer: Yes, DocuSeal provides an Open Source on-premises plan as well as a paid on-premises Pro license for full data control.

faqs.question: Does DocuSeal charge extra for identity verification?
faqs.answer: Yes, ID verification costs $2 per verification and Qualified Electronic Signature costs $2-4 per signature.

faqs.question: Does DocuSeal support AI features?
faqs.answer: DocuSeal's site highlights new AI-native workflow capabilities.

use_cases.title: Individual e-signing
use_cases.description: Individuals can use the free Basic or Open Source plan for legally-binding signatures with indefinite document retention.

use_cases.title: Business compliance and branding
use_cases.description: The Pro plan adds custom branding, automated reminders, SSO/SAML, and bulk sending for organizations with compliance and workflow needs.
use_cases.audience: Businesses in healthcare, financial services, real estate, and education

use_cases.title: Developer-embedded signing
use_cases.description: The signing API and React component let developers embed document signing directly into their own products.

use_cases.title: Data-sensitive self-hosting
use_cases.description: The on-premises Open Source and Pro license plans let organizations run DocuSeal on their own infrastructure for full data control.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Docuseal?
paragraphs: DocuSeal is an open-source document signing platform positioned as an alternative to DocuSign and PandaDoc, supporting legally-binding e-signatures.
It can be deployed as a cloud-hosted service, including a GDPR-compliant EU Cloud option, or self-hosted on-premises, with an open-source edition supported via GitHub.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Docuseal is for
paragraphs: DocuSeal is used by individuals seeking a free e-signature tool as well as businesses in software, healthcare, real estate, education, and financial services that need compliance, branding, and API-driven signing workflows.

enrichment.short_description: BoldSign is an electronic signature platform for sending, tracking, and embedding signing workflows, with an API and CRM integrations.
enrichment.starting_price: $5/month per user (billed annually) on the Growth plan; a free Essential plan is also available
enrichment.seo_meta_description: BoldSign e-signature software: send, track, and embed signing workflows with bulk send, templates, and an API. Free plan and 30-day trial available.

features.title: Bulk sending
features.description: Send documents to hundreds of recipients at once via CSV upload.

features.title: Embedded signing
features.description: Embed signing links and workflows into your own app without writing custom signing UI code.

features.title: AI field detection
features.description: Automatically detects and places signature and form fields on uploaded documents (Business plan and above).

features.title: Signer verification
features.description: Includes ID verification and authentication options to reduce signing fraud.

features.title: Templates
features.description: Reusable document templates, with template sharing available on higher-tier plans.

features.title: Custom branding
features.description: Add a custom logo, colors, and personalized email messaging to the signing experience.

features.title: Developer API and SDKs
features.description: REST API with SDKs for cURL, C#, Java, PHP, Python, and Node.js, plus webhook support.

features.title: Pre-built integrations
features.description: Connects with Salesforce, HubSpot, Microsoft OneDrive, Power Automate, Zapier, and Xero.

pros: Offers a genuine free tier (Essential) with no credit card required
30-day free trial available on paid plans
Unlimited envelopes and templates on the Business plan
Broad developer support with multiple language SDKs and webhooks
SOC 2, GDPR, HIPAA, and PCI DSS compliance noted on the site
cons: Free Essential plan is capped at 25 envelopes and 2 templates per month
Advanced features like SSO, custom roles, and AI field detection are gated to the Business plan and above
API access is billed separately, per envelope, on top of app plans

faqs.question: Does BoldSign have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Essential plan is free and includes 25 envelopes and 2 templates per month with no additional users.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial for paid plans?
faqs.answer: Yes, BoldSign offers a 30-day free trial with no credit card required.

faqs.question: Does BoldSign have an API?
faqs.answer: Yes, BoldSign offers a REST API with SDKs for cURL, C#, Java, PHP, Python, and Node.js, priced separately at $0.75 per envelope with a $30/month minimum.

faqs.question: What integrations does BoldSign support?
faqs.answer: BoldSign integrates with Salesforce, HubSpot, Microsoft OneDrive, Power Automate, Zapier, and Xero.

faqs.question: Is BoldSign compliant with security standards?
faqs.answer: The site states BoldSign is SOC 2, GDPR, HIPAA, and PCI DSS certified, and uses AES-256 encryption.

use_cases.title: Contract signing for growing teams
use_cases.description: Send and track contracts with unlimited envelopes and templates plus team management features.

use_cases.title: Embedded signing in a product
use_cases.description: Use embedded signing links and the API/SDKs to add e-signature workflows directly into an existing application.

use_cases.title: High-volume outbound agreements
use_cases.description: Use bulk CSV sending to distribute the same document to hundreds of recipients at once.
use_cases.audience: Sales and operations teams sending agreements at scale

long_form_blocks.heading: What is BoldSign?
paragraphs: BoldSign is an electronic signature platform that lets users send, track, and embed signing workflows, according to its website.
It offers a free Essential plan alongside paid Growth, Business, and Premium tiers, plus a separately priced API for developers building embedded signing into their own applications.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who BoldSign is for
paragraphs: BoldSign is positioned for businesses that need bulk sending, custom branding, and integrations with tools like Salesforce, HubSpot, and Zapier, as well as developers who want to embed signing via its API and SDKs.

enrichment.short_description: SignWell is an electronic signature platform for sending legally binding documents for signature, with a free plan and developer API.
enrichment.starting_price: $10/month per sender (billed monthly), $12/month billed annually, on the Light plan
enrichment.seo_meta_description: SignWell e-signature software: send legally binding documents in minutes with templates, an API, and a free plan. Paid plans start at $10/month.

features.title: Document signing
features.description: Send documents for legally binding eSignature, optimized for signing from any device.

features.title: Reusable templates
features.description: Create custom templates with pre-fill capabilities to speed up repeated document sends.

features.title: Document workflows
features.description: Set signing orders and conditional logic for multi-party document routing.

features.title: eSignature API
features.description: REST API with full documentation, white-label embeds for signing, sending, and template editing, plus webhook support.

features.title: Audit trails
features.description: Court-admissible audit trails compliant with ESIGN, UETA, and eIDAS regulations.

features.title: Native and Zapier integrations
features.description: Native integrations with Close and QuickBooks Online, plus Zapier connectivity to 5,000+ apps.

features.title: Notifications and reminders
features.description: Real-time notifications and automatic reminders for pending signatures.

pros: Free plan available with no credit card required
API access included even on the free plan (3 free API docs/month)
No contracts or cancellation fees; can pay monthly and cancel anytime
20% discount for annual billing across paid plans
Unlimited documents on all paid plans
cons: Free plan is limited to 1 sender, 1 template, and 3 documents per month
Additional senders cost extra ($10-15/month each depending on plan)
Enterprise pricing is custom and not published on the site

faqs.question: Does SignWell have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, SignWell offers a free plan with 1 sender, 1 template, and 3 documents per month, requiring no credit card.

faqs.question: How much does SignWell cost?
faqs.answer: Paid plans start at $10/month (billed monthly) or $12/month (billed annually) for the Light plan; the Business plan is $30-36/month.

faqs.question: Does SignWell offer an API?
faqs.answer: Yes, SignWell provides a REST API with documentation, white-label embeds, and webhook support, included at every plan tier with a free monthly API document allotment.

faqs.question: Is SignWell legally binding?
faqs.answer: SignWell states its eSignatures are legally binding and compliant with ESIGN, UETA, and eIDAS regulations.

faqs.question: Are there cancellation fees?
faqs.answer: No, SignWell states there are no contracts or cancellation fees, and monthly plans can be canceled anytime.

use_cases.title: Simple document signing
use_cases.description: Send a small number of documents for signature each month using the free plan.
use_cases.audience: Freelancers and individuals with occasional signing needs

use_cases.title: Team document workflows
use_cases.description: Use signing order, conditional logic, and templates to route documents through multiple signers.
use_cases.audience: Small business teams handling contracts and agreements

use_cases.title: Embedded signing for developers
use_cases.description: Use the eSignature API and white-label embeds to add signing directly into an existing product.
use_cases.audience: Developers building signing into their own applications

long_form_blocks.heading: What is SignWell?
paragraphs: SignWell is an electronic signature platform for sending documents for legally binding signature, according to its website, with plans ranging from a free tier to custom Enterprise pricing.
It includes a developer API with webhook support and white-label embeds, available starting on the free plan.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who SignWell is for
paragraphs: SignWell is positioned for individuals and small businesses needing occasional or ongoing document signing, as well as developers who want to embed signing workflows via its API.

enrichment.short_description: SignRequest is an ISO 27001-certified electronic signature platform for signing, sending, and tracking documents online.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: SignRequest: ISO 27001-certified e-signature tool with a free plan, unlimited signatures, and Slack, Zapier, and Salesforce integrations.

features.title: Multiple signing methods
features.description: Sign by uploading an image, drawing with the mouse, typing a name, or using an auto-generated signature.

features.title: Document preparation
features.description: Upload PDF, Word, or Excel files and prepare them for signing.

features.title: Templates
features.description: Create reusable templates for recurring documents (5 on Professional, unlimited on Business).

features.title: Audit trail
features.description: Every document includes a secure audit trail and signing log.

features.title: Team collaboration
features.description: Team accounts with customizable branding and email notifications on signing status.

features.title: Bulk send and API
features.description: Business plan adds bulk send, API access, and Single Sign-On.

features.title: Integrations
features.description: Connects with Google Apps, Slack, Zapier (2,000+ apps), Salesforce, and premium integrations like M-Files and Topdesk.

pros: Free plan available with 10 documents per user per month
Unlimited signatures on all plans
14-day free trial on paid plans
ISO 27001 certified and recognized as a G2 Leader
Broad integration support including Salesforce, Slack, and Zapier
cons: Free plan is capped at 10 documents per user per month
API access and Salesforce/SSO integrations require the top-tier Business plan
Fair Use Policy applies to 'unlimited' documents, with separate bundles for excessive volume
Founding year, headquarters, and company size are not disclosed on the site

faqs.question: Does SignRequest have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Free Plan allows 10 documents per user per month with sign-on-any-device support and Google integrations.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial for paid plans?
faqs.answer: Yes, both the Professional and Business plans include a 14-day free trial.

faqs.question: What does the Business plan add over Professional?
faqs.answer: The Business plan adds unlimited teams and users, unlimited templates, bulk send, API access, Salesforce integration, and Single Sign-On.

faqs.question: What integrations does SignRequest support?
faqs.answer: SignRequest integrates with Google Apps, Slack, Zapier (2,000+ apps), Salesforce, and premium integrations such as M-Files and Topdesk on the Business plan.

faqs.question: Is SignRequest ISO certified?
faqs.answer: Yes, SignRequest is ISO 27001 certified and has been recognized as a G2 Leader.

use_cases.title: Individual document signing
use_cases.description: Sign contracts and documents quickly without printing, signing, or scanning.

use_cases.title: Small team contract workflows
use_cases.description: Teams of up to 5 users manage signing with custom branding and templates on the Professional plan.

use_cases.title: Enterprise signing with Salesforce/SSO
use_cases.description: Larger organizations use bulk send, API access, and Salesforce/SSO integration on the Business plan.

features: Public signing link and custom landing pages

long_form_blocks.heading: What is SignRequest?
paragraphs: SignRequest is an electronic signature platform that lets users sign contracts and other documents without printing, signing by hand, and scanning.
It offers a free plan for occasional use as well as paid Professional and Business plans with templates, bulk send, and integrations such as Salesforce, Slack, and Zapier.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who SignRequest is for
paragraphs: SignRequest suits individuals and small teams who need occasional document signing on the Free plan, as well as growing businesses that need templates, custom branding, and bulk sending on the Professional and Business plans.
Organizations needing API access, Salesforce integration, or Single Sign-On can use the Business plan.

name: Yousign
skip_reason: yousign.com and yousign.fr both 301-redirect to youtrust.com, which displays a banner stating 'Yousign becomes Youtrust' — the product has rebranded away from the Yousign name, so it can no longer be verified/enriched as 'Yousign'.

enrichment.short_description: Oneflow is a Swedish contract lifecycle management platform with e-signatures, workflow automation, and AI-powered contract insights.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Oneflow: contract lifecycle management with e-signatures, workflow automation, and AI-powered contract data insights.

features.title: Digital contract repository
features.description: Centralizes contracts in a structured, searchable repository with clause and obligation tracking.

features.title: Workflow automation
features.description: Automates contract approvals and compliance checks integrated with business systems.

features.title: E-signature and eID signing
features.description: Supports electronic signatures, signing order, and eID authentication (including BankID).

features.title: Oneflow AI
features.description: AI features including Write with AI, AI Extract, AI Summary, AI Review, and AI Insights for contract data.

features.title: 500+ integrations
features.description: Connects with CRM, HR, and productivity tools including Salesforce, HubSpot, Microsoft Dynamics 365, Slack, and Zapier.

features.title: Contract templates
features.description: Provides ready-made templates such as Sales Agreement, NDA, and Employment Agreement.

pros: 500+ native integrations with CRM, HR, and productivity tools
AI-powered contract extraction, summarization, and review features
eIDAS, GDPR compliant with ISO 27001, ISO 14001, and ISO 9001 certifications
Free tier for basic send, track, and sign of contracts
Supports eID authentication including BankID
cons: Paid plans require annual billing only; no monthly billing option
Entry paid plan limited to 5 seats at €250/month
Enterprise pricing requires contacting sales for a custom quote

faqs.question: Does Oneflow offer a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, Oneflow offers a free option to send, track, and sign contracts, alongside paid plans with additional features.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial for Oneflow's paid plans?
faqs.answer: Yes, Oneflow offers a 14-day free trial with no credit card required, giving access to full platform functionality.

faqs.question: How much does Oneflow cost?
faqs.answer: The Business plan starts at €250/month for 5 seats, billed annually. Enterprise pricing is custom and requires contacting sales.

faqs.question: Does Oneflow have AI features?
faqs.answer: Yes, Oneflow AI includes Write with AI, AI Extract, AI Summary (Business plan), and AI Review and AI Insights (Enterprise plan).

faqs.question: What integrations does Oneflow support?
faqs.answer: Oneflow supports 500+ integrations, including Salesforce, HubSpot, Microsoft Dynamics 365, Slack, Zapier, and SAP.

use_cases.title: Sales contract management
use_cases.description: Automates proposal, quoting, and contract signing workflows integrated with CRM systems.

use_cases.title: HR agreement workflows
use_cases.description: Manages employment agreements and onboarding paperwork with e-signature support.

use_cases.title: Enterprise contract governance
use_cases.description: Provides SSO, SCIM, audit logs, and custom admin roles for large-scale contract oversight.

features: Oneflow AI (Write with AI, AI Extract, AI Summary)

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Oneflow?
paragraphs: Oneflow is a digital contract lifecycle management platform, headquartered in Stockholm, Sweden, that centralizes contract creation, e-signature, and lifecycle tracking in one place.
The platform includes Oneflow AI features for extracting, summarizing, and reviewing contract data, plus over 500 integrations with CRM, HR, and productivity tools.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Oneflow is for
paragraphs: Oneflow suits sales, HR, and legal teams that need to automate contract workflows and e-signatures, from individuals using the free tier to enterprises requiring SSO and custom admin controls.

enrichment.short_description: Scrive is a Stockholm-based e-signature and eID platform offering eIDAS-compliant signing, identity verification, and web forms.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Scrive: eIDAS-compliant e-signature and eID platform for agreements, identity verification, and digital onboarding in the EU.

features.title: Electronic signature products
features.description: Offers eSign Online, eSign GO, and eSign API for signing agreements via portal, managed service, or API.

features.title: Qualified Electronic Signatures (QES)
features.description: Provides eIDAS-compliant Qualified Electronic Signatures as a QTSP (Qualified Trust Service Provider).

features.title: eID Hub and ID Check
features.description: Digital identity verification and customer authentication services.

features.title: Web forms builder
features.description: Fill-and-sign web forms to collect data and signatures in a single digital flow.

features.title: Template-based workflows
features.description: Template-based document workflows for repeatable agreement processes.

features.title: Scrive MCP server (beta)
features.description: A beta feature combining generative AI with verified identity and eIDAS-compliant signatures.

pros: Qualified Trust Service Provider (QTSP) under EU eIDAS regulation
ISO 27001 certified with EU-based data hosting
Serves 13,000+ customers across 60+ countries
Offers both API and portal-based signing options
14-day free trial with no credit card required
cons: Public pricing details are not published on the website; requires contacting sales
Product lineup is split across multiple separate offerings (eSign Online, eSign GO, eSign API, eID Hub, ID Check, Forms), which can complicate plan selection
MCP/generative AI features are still in beta

faqs.question: Is Scrive compliant with EU signature regulations?
faqs.answer: Yes, Scrive is a Qualified Trust Service Provider (QTSP) under eIDAS and offers Qualified Electronic Signatures (QES).

faqs.question: Does Scrive offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, Scrive offers a 14-day free trial with no credit card required.

faqs.question: How much does Scrive cost?
faqs.answer: Scrive does not publish specific plan pricing; separate pricing tiers exist for eSign Online, eSign GO, eSign API, eID Hub, ID Check, and Forms, and pricing must be requested from Scrive.

faqs.question: When was Scrive founded?
faqs.answer: Scrive was founded in 2010 in Stockholm, Sweden by two university friends.

faqs.question: Does Scrive have an API?
faqs.answer: Yes, Scrive offers eSign API for integrating electronic signatures directly into other systems.

use_cases.title: Automotive sales agreements
use_cases.description: Used for signing vehicle sales contracts, with Scrive noting a significant share of cars sold in the UK are signed using its platform.

use_cases.title: Customer identity verification
use_cases.description: Verifies and authenticates customers digitally via eID Hub and ID Check during onboarding.

use_cases.title: Digital agreement signing
use_cases.description: Enables businesses to sign agreements online through a portal, API, or managed service.
use_cases.audience: Enterprises and SMBs across the EU

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Scrive?
paragraphs: Scrive, founded in 2010 and headquartered in Stockholm, Sweden, is a Qualified Trust Service Provider offering eIDAS-compliant electronic signatures, identity verification, and web forms.
The platform serves more than 13,000 customers across 60+ countries, processing over 100 million transactions per year, with clients including Volkswagen, Volvo, and Samsung.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Scrive is for
paragraphs: Scrive is built for European businesses that need eIDAS-compliant signing and identity verification, from automotive dealerships to financial services conducting digital customer onboarding.

enrichment.short_description: Penneo is a Copenhagen-based digital signing platform for legally binding e-signatures using European national eIDs like MitID and BankID.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Sign documents digitally with Penneo, a European e-signature platform supporting MitID, BankID, and other national eIDs across the EU.

features.title: National eID signing
features.description: Sign documents using nationally recognized eIDs including MitID/MitID Erhverv (Denmark), BankID (Norway/Sweden), itsme/.beID (Belgium), and AusweisApp (Germany), plus passport signing via ID Verifier.

features.title: eIDAS-compliant signatures
features.description: Supports both Qualified Electronic Signatures (QES) and Advanced Electronic Signatures (AdES) as defined by the EU eIDAS regulation.

features.title: Signing flow automation
features.description: Automate document signing processes through configurable signing flows.

features.title: Document management
features.description: Manage documents and collect data through form-based document workflows, with a document archive included.

features.title: Tamper-proof audit trails
features.description: Every transaction generates a tamper-proof audit trail for compliance and verification.

features.title: Identity validation
features.description: Includes identity validation services alongside signing.

features.title: Open API and integrations
features.description: Offers an open API and pre-built integrations, with API sandbox access on the Pro plan.

pros: Used by 3,500+ companies across Europe
84% customer satisfaction, versus a 75% industry average cited on the site
Average support response time of about 10 minutes
Onboarding completed in under 5 business days
Broad support for national eIDs across multiple European countries
cons: No free plan — only a free trial is offered
Pricing varies by country/region and isn't a single global rate
Cancellation requires 3 months' notice
Strongest adoption and eID coverage is concentrated in Nordic/European markets

faqs.question: Does Penneo offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, Penneo offers a free trial upon request; there is no free plan.

faqs.question: What signature types does Penneo support?
faqs.answer: Penneo supports both Qualified Electronic Signatures (QES) and Advanced Electronic Signatures (AdES) under the EU eIDAS regulation.

faqs.question: Which national eIDs work with Penneo?
faqs.answer: Penneo supports MitID/MitID Erhverv, BankID, itsme/.beID, AusweisApp, and passport verification via ID Verifier.

faqs.question: How many signatures are included in a plan?
faqs.answer: All plans include 500 signatures annually, with additional signatures billed at a per-signature rate and volume discounts available.

faqs.question: What is Penneo's cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellation requires 3 months' notice, and standard payment terms are 14 days.

use_cases.title: Signing audited financial documents
use_cases.description: Accounting and audit firms use Penneo to collect legally binding signatures on annual reports and audit documentation.

use_cases.title: Financial and banking agreements
use_cases.description: Finance and banking organizations use Penneo's eID-based signing for regulated document workflows.

use_cases.title: Legal document execution
use_cases.description: Legal service providers use Penneo to execute contracts and legal documents with qualified electronic signatures.

use_cases.title: Real estate transactions
use_cases.description: Real estate businesses use Penneo to manage signing for property-related documentation.

use_cases.title: HR and administrative paperwork
use_cases.description: HR and administration teams use Penneo to handle employee-related document signing.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Penneo?
paragraphs: Penneo is a certified digital trust platform based in Copenhagen, Denmark, that lets businesses send, sign, and manage documents digitally with legally binding signatures.

It supports nationally recognized eIDs such as MitID (Denmark), BankID (Norway/Sweden), itsme/.beID (Belgium), and AusweisApp (Germany), and complies with the EU eIDAS regulation for both Qualified and Advanced Electronic Signatures.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Penneo is for
paragraphs: Penneo is used by over 3,500 companies across Europe, with particularly strong adoption in Denmark, Norway, Belgium, Germany, and Sweden, spanning audit & accounting, finance & banking, legal services, real estate, and HR.

enrichment.short_description: Concord is an AI-powered contract lifecycle management platform that manages contracts from intake through renewal.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Concord is an AI-powered CLM platform managing contracts from intake to renewal, used by legal, procurement, and sales teams.

features.title: AI Copilot
features.description: AI automatically alerts users about deadlines, explains contract risks and obligations, and suggests edits.

features.title: Executive-ready reporting
features.description: AI builds executive-ready reports on contracts in minutes.

features.title: Redlining and version control
features.description: Built-in redlining, version control, and commenting for contract collaboration.

features.title: E-signature
features.description: Unlimited e-signatures are included across all plans.

features.title: Self-serve contract intake
features.description: Business teams can create compliant contracts on demand using legal-approved templates.

features.title: Integrations
features.description: Connects with tools including Salesforce, HubSpot, Google Drive, Box, Dropbox, and Zapier.

features.title: Approval workflows
features.description: Business-tier and above plans include configurable approval workflows.

features.title: API access and clause library
features.description: Enterprise plan includes API access, a clause library, bulk send, and custom branding.

pros: AI Copilot included in every pricing tier
Unlimited e-signatures on all plans
Wide range of native integrations (Salesforce, HubSpot, Google Drive, Box, Dropbox, Zapier)
Used by thousands of companies, per customer testimonials on the site
Volume discounts available for larger organizations
cons: No free plan is offered, only a free trial
Entry-level Essentials plan starts at $499/month, higher than many CLM competitors
Additional users cost extra on top of the 5 included per plan
API access, clause library, and custom branding are limited to the Enterprise tier

faqs.question: Does Concord offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, Concord offers a free trial; the pricing page includes an FAQ addressing whether a credit card is required to start one.

faqs.question: Does Concord have a free plan?
faqs.answer: No, Concord does not offer a free plan, only paid plans with a free trial option.

faqs.question: How many users are included in each plan?
faqs.answer: Each plan (Essentials, Business, Enterprise) includes 5 users, with additional users billed per month at rates that increase by tier.

faqs.question: What integrations does Concord support?
faqs.answer: Concord integrates with Salesforce, HubSpot, Google Drive, Box, Dropbox, and Zapier, with deeper integrations available starting at the Business tier.

faqs.question: When was Concord founded?
faqs.answer: Concord launched its first end-to-end CLM product in 2014.

use_cases.title: Legal contract review
use_cases.description: Legal teams use Concord's AI to review contracts, understand risks and obligations, and manage redlining and version control.

use_cases.title: Procurement contract intake
use_cases.description: Procurement teams use self-serve intake with legal-approved templates to generate compliant contracts on demand.

use_cases.title: Sales agreement management
use_cases.description: Sales teams use Concord to manage customer agreements with e-signature and deadline tracking.

use_cases.title: Executive contract reporting
use_cases.description: Finance and operations leaders use AI-built reports to get executive-ready visibility into contract portfolios.

features: Integrations (Salesforce, HubSpot, Google Drive, Box, Dropbox, Zapier)

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Concord?
paragraphs: Concord is an AI-powered contract lifecycle management (CLM) platform that manages contracts from intake to renewal in one place.
It launched its first end-to-end CLM product in 2014 and has since introduced 'Horizon,' described on the site as an all-new AI-native platform.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Concord is for
paragraphs: Concord serves Legal, Procurement, Finance, Sales, HR, Operations, and IT teams, and is used by thousands of companies across industries including construction, robotics, and education.
